title: Patriots lose new tight end for 2026 season due to injury
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Julian Hill placed on injured reserve by the Patriots, ending his 2026 season before it starts.
Hill, 25, signed a three-year, $15 million deal with the Patriots in March to be their backup tight end.
Third-round pick Eli Raridon may see an increased role following Hill's injury; other options include CJ Dippre and Tanner Arkin.

title: 5 clues A.J. Brown dropped about his desire to join the Patriots
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: A.J. Brown completed a trade to the Patriots, fulfilling his childhood dream of joining the team.
Brown's fondness for the Patriots was evident when he changed his profile picture to Tom Brady in 2024.
A signed Patriots jersey from Tom Brady and interactions with Julian Edelman hinted at Brown's team interest.

title: Coolidge Corner Theatre workers reject ‘last and final’ contract proposal, plan to announce strike date
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Coolidge Corner Theatre workers in Brookline reject management's final contract offer after two years of negotiations.
Sixty-seven percent of union members voted against the proposal, citing concerns over wages and scheduling.
Union plans to strike, but no date set; hopes remain for management to address workers' demands for a fair contract.

title: 5 things to know about new Patriots wide receiver A.J. Brown
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: A.J. Brown, a Patriots fan growing up, joins New England in a trade from the Philadelphia Eagles.
The Patriots acquired Brown by sending a 2028 first-round pick and 2027 fifth-round selection to the Eagles.
Brown previously won a Super Bowl with the Eagles in February 2025 and is familiar with coach Mike Vrabel.

title: Patriots acquire A.J. Brown in blockbuster trade with Eagles
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Patriots acquire wide receiver A.J. Brown from the Eagles, giving up 2028 first-round and 2027 fifth-round picks.
Brown has surpassed 1,000 receiving yards in six of his seven NFL seasons, bringing dynamic capabilities to Patriots' offense.
Trade executed after June 1 to reduce Eagles' dead cap hit from $43.3 million to $16.3 million, accommodating financial terms.

title: The funeral of Boston firefighter Robert Kilduff Jr., in photos
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: Thousands attended the funeral of Boston firefighter Robert Kilduff Jr. at the Cathedral of the Holy Cross, Boston.
Kilduff Jr. died on May 23 in Dorchester while battling a three-alarm fire, saving two fellow firefighters.
Family, friends, officials including Mayor Michelle Wu, and colleagues spoke at the service honoring Kilduff's sacrifice.

title: A ‘fishy squisher’: NASA confirms meteorite landed in Cape Cod Bay
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: NASA confirmed a meteorite landed in Cape Cod Bay after breaking apart 40 miles above New England.
The meteor caused a sonic boom and released energy comparable to 300 tons of TNT.
Witnesses reported seeing the bright meteor from as far as Ontario, logging over 80 observer accounts.

title: Theo Epstein is reportedly disappointed by Red Sox’ ‘intense’ focus on analytics
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Theo Epstein, a part-owner of Fenway Sports Group, is concerned about Red Sox's heavy reliance on analytics.
Epstein, former Red Sox GM, believes in a balanced approach, combining data with insights from experienced staff.
Red Sox GM Craig Breslow defends his strategy, emphasizing disciplined decision-making and information synthesis.

title: Here’s what Boston meteorologists are saying about warmer temperatures this week
author: Heather Alterisio
bullets: Boston will experience a warming trend with temperatures rising into the 80s by mid to late week.
The National Weather Service reports a dry stretch through Saturday with potential showers on Sunday.
Boston's Meteorological Spring concluded with above average temperatures and below average precipitation.

title: Watch: The best videos of Saturday’s meteor boom over New England
author: Emily Turner
bullets: A meteor exploded off the New England coast around 2:11 p.m. on Saturday, producing a loud sonic boom.
The event was captured by doorbell cameras, dashcams, and a performer dressed as a blueberry muffin.
NASA reported the meteor fragmented 40 miles over northeast Massachusetts and southeast New Hampshire.
